intro:

Ophioglossum vulgatum (Adder's tongue) is a type of plant in the genus Ophioglossum, which is a type of fern (a vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta). 

The health benefits of the Adder's tongue fern are largely anecdotal and have not been scientifically proven, however, it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, such as to treat rheumatism, fever, and skin conditions.

The bioactive constituents of the Adder's tongue fern include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits.

The most common herbal preparations made from the Adder's tongue fern include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, although it is worth noting that the efficacy and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
